blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Let's have coffee. Seems like I've been living with coffee on my lips lately.
The two entered the café, each holding a cup of coffee, and sat on a bench.
Without much conversation, they idly passed the time watching birds fly by.
Unlike most girls, Amy wasn't the type to enjoy chatter.
Moreover, this silence was the greatest solace she couldn't experience in the graduation class.
The reason the usually talkative Seriel stayed quiet was because she sensed the exhaustion of a warrior emanating from Amy.
Though she had grown thinner, the unique aura of the graduation class radiated from her.
There's a saying that class shapes a person.
For Amy, every day might have been hell, but enduring it had already set her far apart from the advanced class.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That must be part of it. I need to advance quickly too.
Amy, who had been noisily sipping her empty iced coffee, was the first to speak.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: How's Shirone these days?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: I was wondering when you'd ask. Have you really not met him at all? Haven't even heard any rumors?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: No. No time to meet, and the graduation class has different classes than the advanced class. I did hear that Samuel went as a teaching assistant last time.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: I see. Well, something interesting has been happening in Class Five lately.
Amy, who had been biting her straw, turned her head with a questioning look.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Like a fish in water, Seriel began chattering excitedly. The gist of the story was the thrilling rivalry between Shirone and Iruki ahead of the speed gun test.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: He used logs to reduce the units? That's innovative. Though, should I say it's typical of Iruki?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Huh? You know Iruki?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Of course I do. We were classmates. Though I advanced quickly, so I didn't see him much. You know, that thing about him—the Servant Syndrome.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Wow, you really do know him! Did you talk to him? He didn't seem like the type to befriend people.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Yeah. When I first entered Class Five, he came up and talked to me.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: What? He bothered you? Oh no! Then this is a rivalry over a loved one! This is a total scoop!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Don't be ridiculous. He's a bit weird. Some of what he says makes sense, but it's hard to empathize. I think he was trying to do to me what he did to Shirone. He started picking a fight the moment he saw me. I didn't engage. Maybe he lost interest because I'm a girl.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Hmm, I see. So he's the so-called 'genius hunter'? Shirone must've been unlucky to cross paths with him.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Not necessarily unlucky. It's a hurdle everyone must face at some point. Still, it must be tough. It's a targeting test, and if Iruki uses logs, he'll score really high.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: How can you say that? Your boyfriend is in trouble. Even if you can't help, you should at least cheer him on. What if Shirone loses to Iruki?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Losing is part of competition. Why are you making such a fuss?
Amy seemed unfazed, unlike before. Perhaps because she was waging a daily war incomparable to the advanced class.
Seriel couldn't hide her disappointment.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: You've never lost! I want to see the two undefeated legends dating! So hurry up and tell Shirone to hang in there. I'll deliver the message myself.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Enough. Are you going to exaggerate it again? This isn't an advancement test; it's a practical evaluation. Shirone's goal is the graduation class too. It'll be a good experience.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Then at least share some high-score tips for the speed gun. You hold the record, don't you? 987 points.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmm. But any advice I could give, Shirone probably already knows. It's not that I'm indifferent—Shirone is truly exceptional. He's already surpassed 700 points, right? I just benefited from specializing in targeting.
At the mention of no solution, Seriel's face fell. Having witnessed their love from the beginning, she was immersed in the situation as if Shirone were the male lead of a novel.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Can he win, no matter what? Shirone has to win.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmm.
Amy propped her chin on her hand and fiddled with her straw.
Merkoda Iruki. Their first meeting had been unpleasant, but talent was talent.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Just focusing on targeting, it won't be easy. Servant Syndrome is a truly powerful ability. Using logs on top of that would make it incredibly tricky to counter. And Iruki's serious, right? If he had seriously challenged me back then…

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: If… if he had challenged you?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I might've lost.
Seriel was shocked. Amy, who had never lost first place in the advanced class, the one who set unbreakable records in Class Five, was saying she might've lost.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Eek, what do we do? Our Shirone.
Seriel's face twisted into a frown.
The speed gun test was now a day away.
Shirone sat on the floor of his dorm, deep in meditation.
He was counting numbers, but it wasn't a sequence formula.
While he had mastered techniques until now, today he sought the answer to what a sequence formula truly was.
A journey of numbers with a starting point but no destination.
Ten hours passed without him moving a muscle.
As time flowed, Shirone realized the essence of the sequence formula in a realm most humans never reach.
It was like witnessing lightning strike right before his eyes.
When he opened his eyes, dawn was breaking outside.
He hadn't eaten or slept for 24 hours, but he felt no fatigue.
A light too sacred to be called emotion lingered in his eyes.
Shirone looked up at the ceiling, engulfed in sorrow.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What he had felt was a great epiphany, but also a chillingly harsh reality.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is this… truly the truth?
No one answered. But Shirone felt as if he had heard one.
Though there were still 30 minutes left until the test, the training ground was already packed with participants and spectators.
Staff bustled about, reinforcing safety measures and installing scoreboards around the image zone.
Not a single student from Class Seven or Six missed the event.
It was a glimpse of the hierarchical society, eager to impress their seniors.
On the other hand, only those from Class Four interested in Shirone and Iruki's showdown made time to attend.
Among them, the most notable was Seriel, one of Class Four's top students.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Ah! Senior! Hello!
Mark rushed over with the excitement of catching a big fish and greeted her.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Good. All the juniors are here. Well, it was the same in my time.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Don't say such sad things. I'm here solely to celebrate Senior Shirone's victory!

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Oh! That's reassuring! Alright, let's fire up our cheering spirit!
Seriel and Mark secured the best seats with a clear view of the image zone.
Wherever she went, a path opened up, making the front row practically reserved.
Most of the chatter revolved around the showdown between Shirone and Iruki.
Shirone, naturally strong in the Spirit Zone, versus Iruki, skilled in strengthening the zone through sequence formulas.
It was a battle to determine the top talent in Class Five.
The test would be taken simultaneously by both.
The school had adopted this competitive format to maximize students' potential.
For the same reason, participants were scheduled later based on their practice scores.
Naturally, Shirone and Iruki were assigned the last slot, Group 20.
As Etella activated the magic barrier, the scoreboard lit up.
After a 10-minute warm-up, the test began with Group 1.
Though technically a practical evaluation focused on individual scores, the speed gun test was also a fun event for spectators due to its competitive nature.
Participants couldn't help but be conscious of this.
Even if they broke personal records, losing to their groupmate left them with bitter expressions as they stepped down.
It took about an hour to complete up to Group 10.
The highest score so far was 533 points, while Nade finished slightly above the average with 491.
As time passed, the students' eyes grew livelier.
They knew what the main event of the day was.
Finally, Group 19 finished, and Etella called Shirone and Iruki.
The clamor of cheers fell silent in an instant.
Shirone and Iruki walked up the steps side by side, not looking at each other.
Their backs radiated competitiveness.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Wow! Senior Shirone looks fired up! I heard Iruki's way ahead in practice.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Pfft. Shirone's strong in actual combat! Remember the teleportation test?
When Seriel glared, Mark laughed awkwardly and scratched his head.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: You have one minute. The test starts when the red light turns on. The magic barrier ensures no interference, so treat it as an individual event. Especially you two—the juniors are watching, so don't embarrass yourselves.
As if conserving even the mental energy required to respond, Shirone and Iruki only nodded.
Etella spread her arms, and the two split off to their respective zones.
The juniors watched intently, not wanting to miss a moment of the showdown.
The tension was palpable.
With both holding records above 700 points, high-speed targeting was expected from the start.
Shirone calmly gazed ahead.
His heart raced, but he felt no tension—a sign of peak human concentration.
As the scoreboard's standby light turned on, he counted down in his mind.
Start!
The moment the red light flashed, Shirone's eyes widened as he initiated the sequence formula.
He hit zero instantly, and numbers surged at an incredible pace.
Whoosh whoosh whoosh—Bam!
Ten photon outputs per second.
Light streaked across all 360 degrees.
Every target in the first step was obliterated.
Iruki's evasive Spirit Zone also scraped through space, eliminating targets.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Whoa! They're both insanely fast! Everything's gone already!
The same happened in the second and third steps.
Targets were destroyed the moment they appeared.
By the sixth step, still tied, Seriel clenched her fists.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Yes! Keep this up, and the outcome's anyone's guess.
Shirone and Iruki matched each other up to the tenth step.
But the next step had 20 targets appearing at once—a number neither could handle in one breath.
The real battle began now.
The scores were 239 to 237, with Shirone slightly ahead.
At this rate, surpassing 700 points was guaranteed.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Yes! He's holding his own against a serious Iruki. Keep pushing!
In targeting, a single mistake could widen the gap drastically.
But the Flash Shirone and evasive Iruki missed nothing.
At the 30-second mark, Iruki tied the score.
Sensing danger, Shirone accelerated his sequence formula.
His Spirit Zone strengthened, and the flashes chained faster.
Shirone took a 4-point lead, making Iruki restless.
Unlike his usual composed self, he was now giving his all.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: And I'm still falling behind? My calculation speed…
Just three days ago, Iruki's victory seemed certain.
Improving sequence formulas in such a short time was impossible.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I can do this! My strategy's working!
Shirone matched Iruki's speed by abandoning modular limits.
Instead of bundling numbers in 100s, he now processed them randomly—232, 187, 99, 276.
It was a risky trade of stability for speed, but his defensive mental endurance kept his focus steady.
For the first time, Shirone pulled ahead significantly, electrifying the crowd.
Even Etella couldn't look away, gripped by the unpredictable tension.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: He abandoned modular limits? He doesn't care about the test score. He's gambling everything to win.
Iruki's lips curled.
He had waited so long for this moment—a rival who could push him to his limits.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Shirone, my gamble is on another level.
Iruki raised the difficulty of his sequence formula.
The change was immediate, leaving students bewildered.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Huh? What's that?
A secondary rhythm emerged amidst the explosions.
Iruki not only caught up but overtook Shirone.
Achieving this required more than double the speed.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Senior Seriel! What's happening? Did Senior Shirone make a mistake…?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: No. Shirone's score is still rising. It's more like Iruki's targeting suddenly got faster.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: How's that possible? He jumped 100 points in an instant.
Seriel, scrutinizing Iruki's performance, finally realized and bit her lip.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: ...He created another Spirit Zone.
The students turned to Iruki.
Two afterimages were now flying at different angles.
Double Spirit Zone was a unique trait of Servant Syndrome, enabling mental division.
It wasn't a technique but an innate ability, impossible to learn.
But what truly shocked Seriel was that Iruki's speed kept increasing despite the additional zone.
Performing two separate sequence formulas simultaneously—could the human mind handle such calculations?
Iruki proved it possible.
And he was doing it with logs, no less.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: As Amy said, he's a monstrous talent. No, beyond talent—a mutant outside human bounds.
The scoreboard reflected Servant Syndrome's power.
A gap of over 100 points, with Iruki several steps ahead.
Once steps diverged, catching up became impossible—the gap only widened.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Faster. Even faster.
Shirone grew desperate.
His modular bundles now exceeded 300 numbers.
The irregularity made calculations more complex and chaotic.
Shirone broke his personal record.
But Iruki wasn't just surpassing his own—he was on track to overtake Amy's.
At this rate, it was inevitable.
Only one option remained for Shirone.
A realm unreachable by mere speed—only by leaping beyond.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But was it possible? Truly?
Again, no one answered.
But Shirone felt as if he had already heard.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Transcend numbers!
